title: Famous Theorems
editorialSummary: Four theorem clusters grouped by the mathematics branch they belong to, with two major false trails: (1) Euler appears twice — Euler's Formula belongs in geometry (V-E+F=2) while Euler's Theorem belongs in number theory (a^φ(n)≡1), and players who confuse these will misplace both; (2) the Prime Number Theorem is a major result in number theory and players reasoning from calculus-sounding language may misplace it.

meta.gptRationale: Some theorem names are very famous, but distinguishing by branch gets harder because names like Euler and theorem-style wording create strong false trails.
